ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status For Closing Of The Estate
Off Calendar May-14-2008 Continued To Jun-11-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case #246689 (Estate of Helen C. Simpers).

Appearances: John Cullen for Wilkie Courter (Petitioner on the Courter matter). Burton McGovern for Wilkie Courter (Petitioner on the Simpers matter).

Mr. McGovern stated that the petition for Final Distribution had been filed for the Simpers matter, but that the Court changed the order from Final Distribution to Preliminary Distribution. In addition, he stated that he responded to the Examiner's notes, that he would not be able to obtain all receipts as one of the beneficiaries was deceased and that the decedent's Counsel was medically unable to communicate. Furthermore, he stated that the Son was waiving commissions on the father's estate and that Mr. Cullen and himself were sharing the attorney's fees of $2,800.00. The Court granted the petition, (subject to a written order).

With regard to the Courter matter, the Court was informed that a new petition for final distribution was going to be filed. The matter was continued to June 11, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Obi.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status For Filing Of Additional Information Requested By The Court
Off Calendar Aug-29-2007 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: John Cullen and Burton McGovern appearing for executor. Mr. McGovern state the creditor's claim was a judgment became a lien on the property. When the property was sold, the lien was pay out of escrow. The Deed of Trust was also pay out of escrow. He argued that this estate is ready to be closed. The distribution from the Simpras estate will be small and it can be dealt with the omnibus clause in the order. Matter submitted.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto CSR# 5229. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: A. Aguerre.

Mr. Cullen requested for preliminary distribution in this estate. Court will allow preliminary distribution. Matter submitted. Court is setting a status hearing on 8/22 in the Simpers estate, case number 246689.
